task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The outer square plate is drawn
task_completion.visual_evidence: A square shape is visible in the LibreCAD drawing workspace.
A large circle is drawn in the center of the square.

task_completion.description: The 8 bolt holes are drawn in a circular pattern
task_completion.visual_evidence: Exactly 8 smaller circles are evenly spaced in a ring around the central hole, inside the square.

task_completion.description: The drawing is saved as bolt_circle_plate.dxf in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'bolt_circle_plate.dxf' and the path '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/', or the LibreCAD window title bar updates to show this filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking on LibreCAD tools (e.g., line, circle, command line within LibreCAD) and interacting with the drawing canvas, rather than using terminal commands to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent did not write the DXF file manually in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing process happens visually within the LibreCAD application, not by typing raw DXF code in a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.
The shapes (square, central circle, bolt holes) appear sequentially across multiple screenshots, proving the agent drew them rather than loading a pre-made file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The plate must be a square (300x300). The central hole must have a radius of 75mm. There must be exactly 8 bolt holes, each with a radius of 6mm. The bolt holes must be arranged on a circular path with a radius of 115mm, spaced 45 degrees apart, with the first hole at the 3 o'clock position (0 degrees). The final output must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/bolt_circle_plate.dxf.
